items.title: Accelerating Structure-Property Relationship Discovery with Multimodal Machine Learning and Self-Driving Microscopy
items.abstract: Microscopy combined with local spectroscopy is widely used to correlate nanoscale structure with functional properties in materials, but conventional measurements rely heavily on human-selected sampling locations and predefined targets, limiting dataset diversity and the potential for discovery. Here, we present a framework that integrates autonomous microscopy with a dual-novelty deep kernel learning (DN-DKL) for adaptive data acquisition and a dual variational autoencoder (VAE) for representation learning. DN-DKL actively guides the microscopy toward structurally and spectroscopically novel regions, enabling efficient collection of large spectral datasets. Dual-VAE embeds local structure and spectroscopic responses into a shared latent manifold that serves as a structure-property relationship map. We applied this framework for the investigation of halide perovskite films using conductive atomic force microscopy. The results reveal distinct hysteresis behaviors that are linked to specific nanoscale structural motifs, including grain boundary junction points that show hysteresis under different bias conditions and asymmetric grain boundaries that suppress charge transport. This framework establishes a general strategy that leverages the complementary strengths of self-driving microscopy, machine learning, and human expertise to accelerate scientific discovery in functional materials.

items.title: GPUMDkit: A User-Friendly Toolkit for GPUMD and NEP
items.abstract: Machine-learned interatomic potentials have revolutionized molecular dynamics simulations by providing quantum-mechanical accuracy at empirical-potential speeds. The graphics processing unit molecular dynamics (GPUMD) package, featuring the highly efficient neuroevolution potential (NEP) framework, has emerged as a powerful tool in this domain. However, the complexity of force field development, active learning, and trajectory post-processing often requires extensive manual scripting, imposing a steep learning curve on new users. To address this, we present GPUMDkit, a comprehensive and user-friendly toolkit that streamlines the entire simulation workflow for GPUMD and NEP. GPUMDkit integrates a suite of essential functionalities, including format conversion, structure sampling, property calculation, and data visualization, accessible through both interactive and command-line interfaces. Its modular, extensible architecture ensures accessibility for users of all experience levels while allowing seamless integration of new features. By automating complex tasks and enhancing productivity, GPUMDkit substantially lowers the barrier to using GPUMD and NEP programs. This article describes the program architecture and demonstrates its capabilities through practical applications.

items.matched_query_text: Papers central to 钛合金、钛-石墨烯体系分子动力学模拟与机器学习性质分析, especially work that connects or combines: structure property relationship; property prediction; titanium graphene interface; titanium alloys; molecular dynamics; machine learning for prediction of mechanical thermal and diffusion properties of Ti based materials; molecular dynamics studies of titanium graphene composites and interfaces; molecular dynamics simulation of titanium alloys and Ti based materials.
